metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each child's file_impact must include the eval spec YAML path (e.g., src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml), and at least child 0f8f must explicitly scope creating the code-review overlay eval spec schema and CI registration.
findings.finding: Parent AC2 ('each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') is only partially covered: every child lists 'eval spec + fixtures' in its Files section but no child's file_impact manifest includes the eval YAML file, and no code-review-*.eval.yaml currently exists in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/. The eval harness infrastructure needed to run and validate these evals (file creation, schema conformance, CI registration) is not explicitly owned.
findings.location: child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5, child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f
findings.suggested_fix: Add the eval YAML path to each child's file_impact. In child 0f8f (the gate child), explicitly scope creation of src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml conforming to the existing eval spec schema, and add a note that the proven pattern includes CI discoverability. Subsequent children follow the same pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval mechanism for AC-2: name the harness (e.g. adapt the existing `.eval.yaml` + Inspect-AI seam in `eval_specs/`), describe the gold corpus, the recall scorer, and the no-fire scorer for at least the `tests` overlay that lands first.
findings.finding: AC-2 references 'a passing recall + no-fire eval' for each overlay, but no eval harness or spec infrastructure exists for code-review overlays (eval_specs/ contains plan-review specs only), and the plan does not describe what framework, tooling, or eval-spec format will implement these evals — leaving the verification mechanism for the plan's own acceptance criterion unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-2: 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval format, file path convention (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-{overlay}.eval.yaml`), and fixture schema for the six new overlay evals, or cite the existing harness that will run them.
findings.finding: AC line 2 requires each overlay to 'land with a passing recall + no-fire eval', but no code-review eval spec infrastructure exists in the repo (the eval_specs/ directory contains only plan-review and general reviewer evals), and the plan does not specify where these eval specs live, what format they use, or what the fallback is if the eval step cannot be completed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scope entry: 'src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-{tests,security,performance,api-compat,db-migrations,llm-prompts}.eval.yaml — each with recall (planted-defect) and no-fire (good-change) cases, following the plan-review-finder.eval.yaml format.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC2's subjective 'passing' and 'do not over-suppress' with observable thresholds (e.g., 'each overlay's eval contains ≥X recall test cases all returning expected flag, and ≥Y no-fire cases all returning no-flag; zero no-fire cases suppress a genuine defect').
findings.finding: AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' and 'do not over-suppress' are subjective terms with no observable threshold, making the criterion non-measurable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Example rewrite: 'Each overlay's eval includes ≥5 recall scenarios (each expected to trigger a flag) and ≥3 no-fire scenarios (each expected to produce no flag); all scenarios yield their expected verdict; no no-fire scenario causes a known-real defect to be dropped.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align terminology for the FP half of overlays across all sections — pick one term (e.g. 'FP-guards') and use it consistently in Why, What, AC1, and AC2 so 'no-fire eval' is explicitly described as the test that validates the FP-guards.
findings.finding: Three different terms — 'FP-discrimination' (Why), 'FP-guards' (What/AC1), and 'no-fire' (What body / AC2) — are used for the same concept across sections, creating a terminology mismatch that could confuse an implementer cross-checking AC1 against AC2.
findings.location: Why section ('FP-discrimination'); What section ('FP-guards', 'no-fire eval'); AC1 ('FP-guards'); AC2 ('no-fire eval')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, replace 'passing recall + no-fire eval' with 'passing recall eval and no-fire (FP-guards) eval' to make the correspondence to AC1's 'FP-guards' explicit; update Why to use 'FP-guards' instead of 'FP-discrimination'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs — e.g. a dogfooding run replaying a set of historic PRs and comparing recall/FP counts before and after, or an explicit reviewer sign-off that the new patterns surface issues the old overlays missed.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism links the overlay enrichment to an observable improvement for the end-user (code-reviewer / rebar user): the ACs describe only internal system evals ('passing recall + no-fire eval') with no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric target, or dogfooding step.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Dogfooding: replay N representative past PRs through each enriched overlay and confirm recall improves (≥1 previously-missed issue surfaced) and FP rate does not increase relative to the un-enriched baseline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval mechanism for recall + no-fire checks (e.g., reference an existing harness, name the CI command, or explicitly state that each overlay ships a self-contained eval document with a defined set of test cases).
findings.finding: AC2 references 'a passing recall + no-fire eval' without defining the eval infrastructure or mechanism — an executor cannot tell whether an eval harness already exists, needs to be created, or is simply a manual checklist review, which could block proceeding.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / Scope paragraph ('Each task lands with its overlay's eval')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'Eval format: each overlay ships a co-located `eval.md` containing ≥N recall scenarios and ≥M no-fire scenarios, each with expected verdict; pass = all expected verdicts match.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for each overlay (e.g., a git-tag or snapshot of the pre-enrichment state, plus a stated procedure for narrowing an over-broad FP-guard) so that behavioral regressions in suppression can be reversed without full re-authoring.
findings.finding: Adding FP-guard content to existing overlays can suppress findings that currently fire for real users (a behavioral regression, not a purely additive change), but the plan specifies no rollback/back-out path if post-deployment over-suppression is detected beyond the pre-land eval.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'overlays do not over-suppress' / Scope: six enriched overlay files
findings.suggested_fix: Before landing each overlay, tag the repo (or save the pre-enrichment file content in a versioned snapshot) and document in the plan: 'to roll back overlay X, restore from tag vX-pre-enrich and re-run the eval.' This satisfies the reversibility requirement without a formal expand-contract sequence for advisory content.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'passing' means for the recall + no-fire eval (e.g., reference the existing eval harness/format, specify minimum recall hit-rate and maximum false-suppression rate, or name the script/command that executes the eval).
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion 'each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' lacks an operationalized definition of 'passing' — no threshold, format spec, or reference to an existing eval harness is provided, leaving the end-state-observable proof under-specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to Scope or AC such as: 'Eval format follows `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/eval-format.md` (or equivalent); passing = all recall fixtures trigger AND no VALID-pattern fixture is flagged. Run via `make eval-overlays`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the vocabulary of each named FP-guard protocol ('Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag') either inline in the overlay prompt or in a cited reference document before the task is implemented.
findings.finding: The tests overlay enrichment references three named decision protocols — 'Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', and 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag' — whose value vocabularies are defined nowhere in the codebase. An executor implementing the enriched overlay cannot apply rules whose content is never specified.
findings.location: Plan § What (tests overlay bullet) / AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'ui-artifact' as an LLM-prompt concern category (with at least one concrete exemplar distinguishing it from instruction-locality and contract-drift) before authoring the llm-prompts overlay enrichment.
findings.finding: The llm-prompts overlay enrichment introduces 'ui-artifact' as a domain category alongside 'domain-mismatch' and 'contract-drift', but 'ui-artifact' is an undefined term in the prompt/LLM-contract context — its distinguishing predicate (what makes a finding a ui-artifact vs. a contract-drift finding) is nowhere specified in the repo.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] At least one child (or the tests child as the gate) must explicitly scope a verify step confirming code-review-verify.md is not modified by any overlay enrichment task.
findings.finding: No child explicitly guards that code-review-verify.md remains unmodified. AC3 in every child states the constraint ('overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md') but no child scopes a check, diff gate, or negative file_impact on verify.md — the enforcement mechanism is assumed but unowned.
findings.location: child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5, child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 0f8f's acceptance criteria: 'code-review-verify.md diff is empty after this task lands.' Alternatively, add an explicit note in each child's Files section listing code-review-verify.md as a DO-NOT-MODIFY sentinel.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Adjust priority of children d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119 and a010-aec8-c088-4314 from 2 to 3 to match the other four dependents and reflect their unblockable dependency on 0f8f.
findings.finding: Children d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119 (security) and a010-aec8-c088-4314 (performance) carry priority=2, the same priority level as the gate child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5 that they depend on. Since they cannot start until 0f8f completes, their priority should be lower (priority=3 like the other dependents) to avoid misleading scheduling signals.
findings.suggested_fix: Set priority=3 on d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119 and a010-aec8-c088-4314 to match children 1ead, 3cb3, and f894.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child d71f must specify the file path or ticket reference for the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria that define the hard exclusions in the security overlay.
findings.finding: Child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119 (security) references 'the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria BY NAME' as hard exclusions, but no child or the parent defines where those DET criteria live or how the overlay author locates them. This is a residual/dangling reference with no resolution path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child d71f's description or Files section: the path or reference to where the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria are defined (e.g., .gitleaks.toml, opengrep rules directory, or a named ticket), so the implementer can locate them without ambiguity.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
